# Yarova

> A simulated remote workplace for DevOps and SRE engineers in Canada. Real squad, real Team Lead, real tickets on real infrastructure. Eight weeks of real work, a real GitHub history, then full support to a stable Canadian job.

Yarova is not a course, not a bootcamp, not a tutorial platform. The work is real: real repositories under the Yarova GitHub organization, real cloud infrastructure, real PR reviews, real incident drills.

Founder: Rohith Yadla — Solutions Architect with 10 years of production consulting experience across Morgan Stanley, Salesforce, and the BC Government.

## What Yarova does

- An 8-week real-work experience for DevOps or SRE engineers
- Squad of 4 reporting to a real Team Lead (a senior engineer at a Canadian tech company)
- Real GitHub history: tickets, PRs, code reviews, runbooks
- Full transition support after the batch: resume rebuild, interview prep, background-check support, day-one prep, probation check-ins at 30/60/90 days
- $2,400 CAD one-time, paid before day one of the batch
- Rejoin guarantee: not hired? Rejoin the next batch, free, until you are

## How batches work

- Multiple batches start each month, on different Mondays
- Some batches run 09:00–17:00 PT (standard shift), some run 13:00–21:00 PT (evening shift, for engineers who still hold a day job)
- 4 seats per batch — cap is enforced

## Two tracks

- DevOps & DevSecOps — AWS, Terraform, GitHub Actions, Kubernetes, Docker
- SRE — AWS, Kubernetes, Prometheus, Grafana, PagerDuty

## Who it is for

- Engineers already in Canada (or arriving within 6 months with valid authorization)
- People stuck in the "no Canadian experience" loop
- Career switchers from QA, sysadmin, or IT support moving into DevOps/SRE
- Newcomers from senior engineering roles abroad who need a Canadian reference
